exampleSurfaces: Seraph refactor preview through shared summary-row variant
fixesApplied: Extracted the refactor-page lower proof band into a reusable SeraphSummaryRow variant so the staggered ledger rhythm is now component-level API instead of page-local alignment behavior.
Added Storybook coverage for ledger variants so the reusable footer/proof cadence is inspectable outside the page previews.
Extended DiffFile and DiffFileView with file-level metadata framing and wired that through Storybook so docs/chat/mock consumers can compose richer diff panels without custom wrappers.
residualRisks: The code surface family is still intentionally lightweight compared with a full IDE model; further expansion would be about breadth rather than fixing an active reuse blocker.
Some dark-mode hierarchy remains CSS-led rather than prop-driven, though the current shared surfaces stay theme-aware.
